content: Hi, I'm feeling a bit stuck in my career as a Software QA Lead. I've been doing it for years, but I'm not sure if it's the right fit for me anymore.
I understand. It's normal to feel stuck in a career, especially after doing it for a long time. What are some of the things that are making you question whether it's the right fit for you?

I think it's just the monotony of it all. I've been doing the same thing for so long and I'm not feeling challenged anymore. I've also been recovering from a knee injury, which has made it hard to do some of the things I love, like Brazilian jiu-jitsu.
I'm sorry to hear that you're dealing with an injury. It can be tough to adjust to a new routine, especially when it's due to an injury. How's your recovery going?

It's going slowly, but I'm getting there. I've been doing a lot of physical therapy and I'm starting to feel like myself again. I've even been able to start doing some light speedcubing, which has been a great way to challenge my mind and keep my hands busy.
That's great to hear! Speedcubing is a great way to keep your mind sharp and it's a fun hobby. What kind of goals do you have for your career, now that you're feeling stuck?
I'm not sure, I've been considering a few different options. I've always been interested in board games and I've even thought about starting my own game design company.
That's an interesting idea! Starting your own business can be a lot of work, but it can also be very rewarding. What kind of experience do you have with game design?
I've just been a hobbyist for years, I love playing games and coming up with new ideas. I've even designed a few games with my friends, like a crossword puzzle game.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a passion project that you can work on in your free time. What kind of challenges do you think you might face when starting your own business?
